d2lr_e1_damage_control.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e1_damage_control.text: Yesterday's rough start has people talking — not in a good way. A group of customers are outside looking hesitant.

d2lr_e1_customers.speaker: Hesitant Customer
d2lr_e1_customers.text: My friend said the service here wasn't great yesterday. Is there any reason we should give you a chance?

choices.text: Apologize sincerely and offer a discount
Acknowledge the issue and promise improvement
Defend yourself and explain what happened
Invite them in and let actions speak

d2lr_e1_apologize.speaker: Customer
d2lr_e1_apologize.text: A discount and a genuine apology? We'll give you another shot.

d2lr_e1_promise.speaker: Customer
d2lr_e1_promise.text: We appreciate the honesty. Everyone has bad days. We'll come in and see.

d2lr_e1_defend.speaker: Customer
d2lr_e1_defend.text: Right. So it was the customer's fault? We'll pass.

d2lr_e1_actions.speaker: Customer
d2lr_e1_actions.text: Actions speak louder than words. Alright, let's see what you've got.

d2lr_e1_result_apologize.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e1_result_apologize.text: Genuine apologies with tangible gestures rebuild trust faster than words alone.

d2lr_e1_result_promise.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e1_result_promise.text: Acknowledging mistakes shows maturity. Now follow through — a promise without action makes things worse.

d2lr_e1_result_defend.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e1_result_defend.text: Defensiveness when your reputation is already low accelerates damage. Customers want accountability, not excuses.

d2lr_e1_result_actions.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e1_result_actions.text: Letting your service speak is powerful but risky when trust is low. Make sure the experience delivers.

d2lr_r_bad_review.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_r_bad_review.text: A customer left a scathing online review about your laundromat. It's getting views.

d2lr_r_review_b.speaker: You
d2lr_r_review_b.text: The review complaints about dirty machines and poor service. How do you respond?

choices.text: Ignore it — hope it goes away
Ask happy customers to leave reviews

d2lr_r_review_public.speaker: Online User
d2lr_r_review_public.text: The owner actually responded and apologized. Maybe it's worth another chance.

d2lr_r_review_private.speaker: Reviewer
d2lr_r_review_private.text: A personal message and an apology? I'll update my review.

d2lr_r_review_ignore.speaker: Potential Customer
d2lr_r_review_ignore.text: They never even replied to that review. Doesn't say much about their service.

d2lr_r_review_positive.speaker: Happy Customer
d2lr_r_review_positive.text: Of course! You've been great to us. Happy to share my experience!

d2lr_r_review_result_public.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_r_review_result_public.text: Public responses show accountability. Others see how you handle criticism — that builds trust.

d2lr_r_review_result_private.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_r_review_result_private.text: Personal outreach shows genuine care. Turning a critic into a fan is one of the best outcomes.

d2lr_r_review_result_ignore.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_r_review_result_ignore.text: Silence reads as indifference online. Unaddressed negative reviews compound over time.

d2lr_r_review_result_positive.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_r_review_result_positive.text: Smart reputation management! Amplifying positive voices is as important as addressing negative ones.

d2lr_r_machine_theft.speaker: Staff
d2lr_r_machine_theft.text: Boss! Someone broke into a machine and stole the coins. There's damage to the lock.

d2lr_r_theft_b.speaker: You
d2lr_r_theft_b.text: A theft and damaged machine. Other customers are watching how you handle this.

choices.text: Post a warning sign and increase security
Do nothing and hope it doesn't happen again

d2lr_r_theft_police.speaker: Customer
d2lr_r_theft_police.text: Good that they called the police. At least they take security seriously.

d2lr_r_theft_quiet.speaker: Customer
d2lr_r_theft_quiet.text: Did something happen? They're being weird about that machine.

d2lr_r_theft_security.speaker: Customer
d2lr_r_theft_security.text: New cameras and signs? They're taking safety seriously now.

d2lr_r_theft_nothing.speaker: Customer
d2lr_r_theft_nothing.text: That broken machine has been like that all day. Don't they care?

d2lr_r_theft_result_police.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_r_theft_result_police.text: Calling authorities shows you take security seriously. Transparency builds trust.

d2lr_r_theft_result_quiet.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_r_theft_result_quiet.text: Hiding problems doesn't make them disappear. Customers notice when something's off.

d2lr_r_theft_result_security.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_r_theft_result_security.text: Visible security measures reassure customers. Proactive protection is good business.

d2lr_r_theft_result_nothing.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_r_theft_result_nothing.text: Inaction during a crisis erodes trust. Customers want to feel safe in your business.

d2lr_r_staff_quit.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_r_staff_quit.text: One of your part-time staff just quit without notice. You're short-handed.

d2lr_r_quit_b.speaker: You
d2lr_r_quit_b.text: A sudden departure leaves you with extra work and fewer team members.

choices.text: Work extra hours to cover the shift

d2lr_r_quit_work.speaker: Staff
d2lr_r_quit_work.text: You're working yourself too hard, boss. The rest of us will do our best.

d2lr_r_quit_overtime.speaker: Staff
d2lr_r_quit_overtime.text: Overtime pay? We'll cover the shifts. Thank you for taking care of us.

d2lr_r_quit_hire.speaker: Applicant
d2lr_r_quit_hire.text: I saw your urgent hiring notice. I'm available to start today.

d2lr_r_quit_reduce.speaker: Customer
d2lr_r_quit_reduce.text: Closed early again? I drove all the way here.

d2lr_r_quit_result_work.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_r_quit_result_work.text: Burning yourself out isn't sustainable. Your team sees it, and it worries them.

d2lr_r_quit_result_overtime.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_r_quit_result_overtime.text: Pay alone won't keep people long-term, but during a crisis, it shows you value their effort.

d2lr_r_quit_result_hire.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_r_quit_result_hire.text: Quick hiring fills gaps, but vetting is still important. A bad hire is worse than no hire.

d2lr_r_quit_result_reduce.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_r_quit_result_reduce.text: Reducing hours protects your team but frustrates customers. Communicate changes clearly.

d2lr_e3_supplier_trust.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e3_supplier_trust.text: Your regular supplier heard about your rough start. They're demanding upfront payment now.

d2lr_e3_supplier.speaker: Supplier
d2lr_e3_supplier.text: Given recent reports about your shop, I'm no longer comfortable with credit terms. Full payment upfront or no deal.

d2lr_e3_pay.speaker: Supplier
d2lr_e3_pay.text: Payment received. Your order will be delivered tomorrow.

d2lr_e3_negotiate.speaker: Supplier
d2lr_e3_negotiate.text: Half upfront is acceptable. Show improvement and we'll return to normal terms.

d2lr_e3_alternative.speaker: New Supplier
d2lr_e3_alternative.text: We can supply you, but our rates are higher. Starting from scratch is never easy.

d2lr_e3_explain.speaker: Supplier
d2lr_e3_explain.text: Coming to me directly with a plan shows character. Let's keep normal terms — but I'll be watching.

d2lr_e3_result_pay.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e3_result_pay.text: Maintaining supply chains is critical. A business without supplies cannot serve customers.

d2lr_e3_result_negotiate.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e3_result_negotiate.text: Negotiating middle ground preserves relationships while managing cash flow.

d2lr_e3_result_alternative.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e3_result_alternative.text: Switching suppliers under pressure is risky. Sometimes fixing existing relationships is smarter.

d2lr_e3_result_explain.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e3_result_explain.text: Transparency and a clear improvement plan can salvage damaged business relationships.

d2lr_e4_customer_retention.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e4_customer_retention.text: A regular customer is at the counter, looking disappointed. They're thinking of leaving.

d2lr_e4_customer.speaker: Regular
d2lr_e4_customer.text: I've been coming here for a year, but lately, the service has been slipping. I'm considering other options.

choices.text: Apologize and offer a loyalty reward
Ask what we can do to keep them
Let them leave — can't please everyone

d2lr_e4_reward.speaker: Regular
d2lr_e4_reward.text: A loyalty reward? That's thoughtful. I'll stay and see if things improve.

d2lr_e4_listen.speaker: Regular
d2lr_e4_listen.text: Thank you for listening. I hope things get better.

d2lr_e4_ask.speaker: Regular
d2lr_e4_ask.text: Just be consistent. Clean machines and friendly faces are all I ask.

d2lr_e4_let.speaker: Regular
d2lr_e4_let.text: I see how it is. Goodbye.

d2lr_e4_result_reward.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e4_result_reward.text: Rewarding loyalty during tough times shows you value your regulars. Smart retention move.

d2lr_e4_result_listen.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e4_result_listen.text: Listening is the first step. Now follow through with action.

d2lr_e4_result_ask.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e4_result_ask.text: Asking what customers need shows humility and care. Now deliver on their requests.

d2lr_e4_result_let.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e4_result_let.text: Losing a regular is expensive. One lost customer costs more than the revenue — it's lost word-of-mouth too.

d2lr_e5_team_recovery.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e5_team_recovery.text: End of a tough day. Your staff is feeling the pressure from the low reputation.

d2lr_e5_staff.speaker: Staff
d2lr_e5_staff.text: Boss, the team is really down. People are saying negative things about us. What's the plan?

choices.text: Hold a team meeting and share recovery plan

d2lr_e5_meeting.speaker: Staff
d2lr_e5_meeting.text: Thank you for being straight with us. Knowing there's a plan makes a huge difference.

d2lr_e5_bonus.speaker: Staff
d2lr_e5_bonus.text: A bonus really helps right now. We'll push harder tomorrow.

d2lr_e5_pressure.speaker: Staff
d2lr_e5_pressure.text: That's easy to say when you're the boss. We feel unsupported.

d2lr_e5_win.speaker: Staff
d2lr_e5_win.text: Three compliments today! Focusing on the small wins really helps.

d2lr_e5_result_meeting.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e5_result_meeting.text: Transparent leadership during hard times builds loyalty. Your team needs direction, not just sympathy.

d2lr_e5_result_bonus.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e5_result_bonus.text: Financial recognition shows care. It costs money now but buys loyalty for the recovery.

d2lr_e5_result_pressure.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e5_result_pressure.text: Dismissing struggles with clichés damages trust. Your team needs empathy and direction.

d2lr_e5_result_win.speaker: Mentor
d2lr_e5_result_win.text: Celebrating small wins reframes adversity as progress. Good leaders find momentum in difficult moments.
